Explanation: The clues point to the Taj Mahal: it is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in India, built by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan, depicted on Indian currency, and a replica of its central dome is outside the Taj Mahal House in the British Museum in London (another continent). The Taj Mahal was inscribed as a World Heritage Site in 1983, over 35 years after India's independence in 1947, which is close to "more than 40 years." It is within 200 miles of Agra Fort, another UNESCO site. All clues fit the Taj Mahal [98528][53021].
Exact Answer: Taj Mahal
Confidence: 95%
